title: pilosella calodon subsp. phyllophora
intro: Pilosella calodon subsp. phyllophora, also known as the Wood Sorrel, is a rare and lesser-known plant species. It belongs to the Rosaceae family and is characterized by its distinctive leaves and stems. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Pilosella calodon subsp. phyllophora are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as potential uses in treating digestive issues and skin conditions.
